segments.text: Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 3, Chapter No. 16, Text No. 29.
Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishlaprabhupada.
The Lord then said to His attendants,
Jaya and Vijaya, depart this place, but fear not.
Though I am capable of nullifying the Brahmanas' curse, I would not do so.
On the contrary, it has my approval.
As explained in connection with Text No. 26,
all the incidents that took place had the approval of the Lord.
Ordinarily, there is no possibility that the four sages
could be so angry with the Dharmasur,
nor could the Supreme Lord neglect His two doorkeepers,
nor can one come back from the Vaikuntha after taking birth there.
All these incidents, therefore, were designed by the Lord Himself
for the sake of His pastime in the material world.
Thus, He plainly says that it was done with His approval.
Otherwise, it would have been impossible for inhabitants of Vaikuntha
to come back to this material world simply because of Brahmanical curse.
The Lord especially blesses the so-called culprits.
A devotee, once accepted by the Lord, can never fall down.
That is the conclusion of this incident.
So, these are the words spoken by Lord to Jaya and Vijaya.
Depart this place, but fear not.
Though I am capable of nullifying the Brahmana's curse,
On the contrary, it has my approval.
all the incidents that took place had the approval of the Lord.
We all have heard of this common phrase,
that is not a blade of grass moves without the Lord.
Without the Lord's approval, not even a blade of grass can move.
Many people who have faith in the Lord,
and when they hear this statement that not a blade of grass moves without the sanction of the Lord,
they find it difficult to reconcile that why there is so much evil in this world.
It is not a blade of grass that moves without the Lord's sanction.
So many innocent people, apparently innocent people are getting killed.
If not a blade of grass moves without the Lord's sanction, then why all this is happening?
So, some people who are skeptical,
Shraddha is there, but it is very tender.
They think that probably things have gone out of control of the Lord also,
The influence of Kali Yuga is so,
maybe things have gone out of control.
Or, maybe the Lord is not so powerful
to deal with Asuras in this Kali Yuga,
or so many of evil minded.
when we see these incidents happening.
Jaya and Vijaya are being cursed.
although on one side the Lord says that,
a devotee of mine will never perish.
Yad Gattva Na Nivartante Taddhamam Paramam Mama.
it would have been impossible for inhabitants of Vaikuntha
to come back to this material world.
To be a doorkeeper of the Lord,
Just like to be a personal assistant,
unless one is an exalted devotee,
coming down to this material world.
So, sometimes these situations are very bewildering.
it is out of control of the Lord.
when one is not a devotee,
it's pure law of karma working.
and in the case of the Four Kumaras,
Lord is not just the witnesser,
Lord plays a very important role,
in the lives of a devotee.
And when the Lord talks about,
witnesser, sanctioner and director. Somebody who is not a devotee just has witnesser and
sanctioner. A third component comes in play in the life of devotees when someone tries
to surrender to the Lord or surrenders to the Lord, the Lord becomes witnesser, sanctioner
and director. Many times devotees may go through some challenges, may not be because of their
karma. It's very intricate to find out but may not be because, just like the Pandavas,
they were so pure, so righteous, completely devoted to the Lord, completely surrendered
but we see that they are facing so many calamities. It has Lord's approval.
Here Jaya and Vijaya are being cursed and they have to fall down. The Lord has approval.
So the Lord has witnesser, sanctioner and director. In case of devotees life, Krishna
directs, even the hitting of karma is directed by the Lord. If according to karma, devotees
are supposed to be very prosperous, very, you know, go through a very pleasant good
Krishna feels that the devotee is not matured to handle that prosperity because sometimes
When too much of bhoga and aishwarya comes,
too much of enjoyment of prosperity comes,
So Krishna may not sanction prosperity or good times for a devotee.
he has to suffer a lot and Krishna feels that he is not ready, his faith is still a little
tender, he may lose faith, he may get shaken, Krishna may not allow that karma to act.
Just like Shri Prabhupada before going to America, he was quote unquote in one way,
it's all the Leela of the Lord, he was meeting so much obstacles, so much struggle, it's
not that Shri Prabhupada was not pure or is just after going to America or after going
to Jaludutta, things changed drastically, no.
So the Lord is, it all had Lord's approval.
Today if we look back, all that struggle even glorified.
We were reading the sacrifice of Shri Prabhupada and his devotion to the Lord.
We were reading Jaludutta diary, Shri Prabhupada having heart attacks.
And today when we read that, our faith in Shri Prabhupada, our conviction, the sacrifice
he took to spread this message, we get so inspired, get so motivated.
If he at the age of 70 years could do all that, what are we?
So, in case of devotees, just like here in the Bhagavad Gita also, we have Arjuna with
Krishna Surya Sama Maya Hai Andhakaar.
Sun and darkness, they don't stand together.
When the sun is high up in the sky, you don't see, don't feel any darkness.
Unless you are in a closed room, all windows closed.
So likewise when Krishna is there, Krishna's company is there, Krishna consciousness is there,
of life. Even Krishna in the second chapter says, how come these impurities have come
upon you? Although Krishna is right there next to Arjuna, how is it possible for Arjuna
to be in such bewilderment? In the fourth chapter, Arjuna asks Krishna, how is it that
you have spoken this knowledge to Sangat Vivasvan when I understand that both of us are contemporaries?
And then Krishna says, both you and me have taken many, many births, you don't remember,
I remember them all. So does Arjuna not have an idea that we are spirit, soul, atma, many,
many births we have taken? A bhakta in ISKCON will also have that understanding. We are
eternal, many births we have taken. So these are all leelas of the Lord. Prabhupada says,
...for the sake of his pastime in the material world, Arjuna was put in dilemma so that Krishna
could authoritatively speak the statements of the Gita, the instructions, for the benefit
So likewise, the Lord enacts various pastimes, approves something which is apparently difficult
for us to reconcile. Just like Pariksit Maharaj getting angry and putting the snake around
that ranamまた keFrom ghat tiri pavadegadiconf geeti ko buigaug, gaanu apata pain understandata
rishi. Pariksit is such an exalted devotee. How could that happen? Or for that matter
Lord Shiva getting attracted or Lord Brahma. So these are all exalted pastimes of the Lord
that the Lord has approved this, although they were exalted devotees. And if we hear
these pastimes in a bona fide parampara, we will be able to understand what the Lord
is trying to instruct to this pastime. What is the learning? If you are not fortunate
to hear these pastimes in a proper bona fide parampara from a bona fide spiritual master,
one can get bewildered. That's why many times people even lose faith in scriptures because
you see it's all very bewildering.
Something is being told here, something is being told there and they don't tally, they
don't reconcile. In Shiva Purana it is said Shiva is supreme, here Bhagavatam is a Krishna
is supreme. So here in the previous verse we have the sages circumambulating the Lord,
the four Kumaras, offered their obeisances and returned extremely delighted at learning
of the divine opulences of the Vaishnava.
So we see that how, although they cursed Jaya and Vijaya, the Lord personally came,
apologised. They realised their mistake and there was a very nice conversation which we
just now read in this chapter and then finally they circumambulate the Lord, offered obeisances
We all are very fortunate by the mercy of a pure devotee of the Lord.
We all have the good fortune of, just like here it is said, the sages circumambulated the Lord,
offered their obeisances and returned extremely delighted.
There are many, many spiritualists, impersonalists, they are trying to perfect their life,
There are many, many spiritualists, impersonalists, they are trying to perfect their life,
There are many, many spiritualists, impersonalists, they are trying to perfect their life,
practising different kinds of yoga, sanas, different processes.
We are very fortunate that in a very bona fide way, according to Pancharathik Vidai,
out of request of a pure devotee, the Lord descends in the form of Archa Vigraha
and the Lord is personally present.
The good fortune what the sages had to circumambulate the Lord,
to have darshan of the Lord, to offer obeisances to the Lord,
we all have that good fortune.
Prabhupada says, you come to the temple, here is Shyama Sundara's form, you can see always.
We have many pastimes where the Lord comes, they meet the devotee, there is a conversation and the Lord departs.
We have many pastimes where the Lord comes, they meet the devotee, there is a conversation and the Lord departs.
We have many pastimes where the Lord comes, they meet the devotee, there is a conversation and the Lord departs.
Whereas, see of our good fortune, the Lord has descended personally in the form of Archa Vigraha,
He is there always for us to go, to have darshan, to offer obeisances, to circumambulate.
You can see always, come as many times as you like.
That's a different thing that we don't do.
That's a different thing that we don't do.
We don't have a value for it.
We have taken it for granted.
In another place Prabhupada says, if we don't have proper consciousness,
even worship of Deity will become Galagraha, a pain in the neck.
If we have proper consciousness, Krishna consciousness,
then we will not see Deity as made of metal, stone,
we will see Krishna personally standing.
in this way the impression of the Lord will be within your mind.
And when you have got this impression,
Seeing Deity always, at least daily,
our temple Deity is like this.
We should understand, every day as part of a schedule,
we have Darshan Aarti, we have Mangal Aarti,
That impression, either you are sitting in one place,
you can always think of this Deity.
So this Deity worship is a benediction,
the Lord has descended in a form,
which we can perceive with our eyes,
and we can capture the impression of the Lord,
because the mind is there in Krishna,
Therefore Deity worship is so essential for neophyte,
the opportunity to think of Supreme Lord,
by the impression of Deity within the mind.
he was going around the compartment checking tickets,
and he came across a wallet, a purse,

and thought maybe one of the passengers would have dropped it.
Just like when we find something,
natural thing is to find out who has dropped it,
and return it to that person.
So he picked up the purse,
just to see if there is some listing card,
so that he can identify who the owner is,
and return the purse to him.
there was hardly any address there,
There was a picture of Krishna.
you know, small window kind of thing.

So then he raised the purse,
is this purse belonged to anyone?
So now he wanted to ascertain,
or he is just simply for the sake of,
what's the harm in getting a purse?
So he says, what's the proof?
What's the identity that this belongs to you?
there is a picture of Lord Krishna.
anyone can have a picture of Krishna in the purse.
why don't you have your picture?
why don't you have your picture in the purse?
let me tell you the reason why I don't have my picture.
my father gifted this purse to me.
When I went to the college,
he used to send lot of money to me,
because I was attached to my father,
I put the picture of my father in the purse.
And that remained for many years,
I became enamoured by my looks.
I had nice thick black hairs,
I replaced the picture of my father with,
I used to spend lot of time,
I got so attached to him.
just to spend time with him,
I would go late to the office,
the child picture was placed in the purse.
My son now has grown up.
He hardly comes to meet me.
that it is only Lord Shri Krishna,
eternal relationship we have with Krishna.

I would have been so lonely today.

and gave the purse back to this person.

if we are conscious of Krishna,
with the miseries of this material world.
Prabhupada gives a very nice example,
He says, just like the lotus leaf,
You put a drop of water,
we all get, so to say,
because of various kinds of attachment,
It is actually out of pain.
We want life independent of Krishna.
We want to enjoy independent of Krishna.
to bring us back to the consciousness, original consciousness, which is Krishna consciousness.
And when we become conscious of Krishna, when we have impression of Krishna,
when we are thinking of Krishna, man-mana, bhav-mad-bhakto, mad-yajik, mam-namaskuru,
all this material consciousness has no place.
Even the so-called miseries, so-called this thing,
the miseries can strike us, can hit us, can affect us, only if we are not Krishna conscious.
It's not that miseries will not be there. In devotees' life also, old age will be there, diseases will be there, death will be there.
But like a lotus leaf, it cannot touch the consciousness. The example is given of Pandavas.
How in spite of all the miseries, they are more and more Krishna conscious,
to the extent that Kuntimaran is saying, Krishna, please sanction more miseries.
So, just like here, the Lord, out of His so, out of His causeless mercy,
His devotees, where one side Krishna says that they do not fall down,
namae bhaktaye pranashyati, Krishna is saying here, all glories unto you.
On one side He is saying glories, and then He is approving them to fall down.
So we also have a very, very good fortune now,
to remember Krishna, be conscious of Krishna, get attached to Krishna.
And when we do that, then all this maya, tamas, andhakaar, darkness, ignorance, attachments, various kinds of miseries,
which are bewildering us, will have no place in our life.
We will be blissful in consciousness of Krishna.
Now, if we know that this is our prerogative, this is what we can,
this state of consciousness we can achieve, that will motivate us.
That will motivate us to be serious in spiritual life.
Be sincere in doing the sadhana which Prabhupada has prescribed, our daily program.
Because the results, what we get in return, or whatever little bit small thing we do,
manmana, think of Krishna, madhyachi, maam namaskru, offer obeisances to me, remember me.
The amount of effort involved is very less compared to what we are going to get in return.
So, when we hear these statements, how the Lord is so dear to His devotees,
how He goes out of His way to protect His devotees,
sometimes for the devotees' sake, for this pastime, He approves something.
Just like here, it's been approved by the Lord.
So, when we have that consciousness of the Lord, we will be very, very, so to say, enthused and enlivened
to render devotional service to Krishna.
Vrantraj shri madbhagavatam ki shri Prabhupada.
